content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN non-negative integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. You are allowed to perform following operation any number of times:


Choose any valid index iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N) and a positive integer M>0M>0M>0 and replace AiA_iAi​ with Ai mod MA_i\bmod MAi​modM. That is, replace AiA_iAi​ with its remainder when divided by MMM, which is a value between 000 and M−1M-1M−1, inclusive.
MMM does not need to be same between different operations.
Find the minimum number of operations to make all the array elements **equal**.

**Note:** Since the input and output are large, prefer using fast input-output methods.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then TTT test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the size of array.
The second line contains NNN integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ - the elements of the array.


Output Format
For each test, print one line containing an integer which denotes minimum number of operations performed to make all the elements equal.
constraints: It is guaranteed that the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In first test case, we can make all the elements equal by following operations:

Take i=1i = 1i=1 and M=2M = 2M=2 and do A1:=A1 mod 2=0A_1 := A_1 \bmod 2 = 0A1​:=A1​mod2=0.
Take i=2i = 2i=2 and M=1M = 1M=1 and do A2:=A2 mod 1=0A_2 := A_2 \bmod 1 = 0A2​:=A2​mod1=0.
Take i=3i = 3i=3 and M=3M = 3M=3 and do A3:=A3 mod 3=0A_3 := A_3 \bmod 3 = 0A3​:=A3​mod3=0.
After these 333 operations we have all the elements equal to zero.

In second test case, we can perform following operation:

Choose i=3i = 3i=3 and M=2M = 2M=2 and do A3:=A3 mod 2=1A_3 := A_3 \bmod 2 = 1A3​:=A3​mod2=1.
After this operation all the elements are 111.
